title: Tabernaemontana hannae
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana hannae is classified within the family Apocynaceae, a large family of flowering plants. This family is further divided into 145 genera, with Tabernaemontana being one of them. Tabernaemontana hannae is a species of flowering plant native to Madagascar. The plant is part of the genus Tabernaemontana, which comprises around 50 species of plants. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Tabernaemontana hannae shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as having a milky sap and showy flowers.

intro: Tabernaemontana hannae, also known as the Hawaiian cowtail vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana hannae include alkaloids, such as voacangine and voacristine, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in herbal remedies such as salves and tinctures to treat various health conditions.
